emergent_themes.description: Ordering food specifically because of viral social media content, creating FOMO-driven consumption
Gap between desired cooking identity (elaborate meals) and actual behavior (convenience-driven)
Technical issues or poor implementation of offers driving permanent platform switching
Frustration with new-customer-only promotions leading to platform preference

coding_summary: P024 reveals a complex relationship between aspirational food identity and convenience-driven reality. Strong social media influence drives specific orders (viral TikTok foods), but day-to-day behavior is primarily fatigue and mood-driven. The transition from student to working adult has increased financial freedom and experimentation, but core triggers remain emotional (tired, craving, treating self). Critical insight: Deliveroo's technical failure with student offers created lasting brand damage, while Uber Eats' inclusive promotion strategy built loyalty. Diary data confirms high takeaway usage despite cooking aspirations.
